description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) end-to-end test driver authoring.

## Problem

`lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex` — per-site `Task.shutdown(task, :brutal_kill)` on a timed-out mutation aborts a task that may be mid-flight inside `:code.load_binary/3` (during the cover-recompile cycle), leaving the Code.Server with an unreleased load lock for the mutated module. Subsequent attempts to load any module in the same BEAM time out / deadlock waiting on the orphaned lock.

## Symptom

In the S7 end-to-end driver, Scenario 4 (`infinite_looper.ex` → `:timeout`) cannot run in the same BEAM as the other scenarios. When it does run, scenarios that follow fail with hangs or with confused module-load errors. Scenarios 5/6/8 (per the original implementer's account) similarly suffer when the mutation-bearing scenarios precede them.

## Spec reference

`mutagen.mutation_pipeline` r4 (per-site timeout classification) + r7 (taint propagation across successors) — both rules require the BEAM stay healthy across timeout events.

## Resolution

Options to investigate:

1. Replace `Task.shutdown(:brutal_kill)` with a cooperative-cancellation path (e.g., a sentinel `receive` checkpoint inside the per-site sandbox, plus a graceful `exit(:timeout)`).
2. Run the per-site sandbox in a peer Erlang node so brutal_kill cannot touch the orchestrator's Code.Server. (Heavier — only if option 1 is not viable.)
3. After brutal_kill, explicitly release the Code.Server's per-module lock via the documented internal API (if one exists).

The fix must keep r4's timeout-classification semantics intact while not leaving the Code.Server poisoned for the next per-site iteration.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 end-to-end suite, Scenario 4 + state isolation in driver)
- mutagen-wrd.11 / mutagen-wrd.12 (sister production-wire-up bugs surfaced in the same audit cycle)
title: DISCOVERY: MutationLoop Task.shutdown(:brutal_kill) corrupts Code.Server load locks
